title: Oracle SQL Cheat Sheet
description: Essential Oracle SQL commands and syntax for database management.

commands.description: Select all columns from a table
commands.scenario: Select all columns from a table

commands.command: INSERT INTO employees (id, name, department) VALUES (1, 'John Doe', 'Sales');
commands.description: Insert a new row into a table
commands.scenario: Insert a new row into a table

commands.command: UPDATE employees SET department = 'Marketing' WHERE id = 1;
commands.description: Update existing records in a table
commands.scenario: Update existing records in a table

commands.command: CREATE TABLE departments (id NUMBER, name VARCHAR2(50));

commands.description: Delete a table from the database
commands.scenario: Delete a table from the database

commands.description: Add a new column to an existing table
commands.scenario: Add a new column to an existing table

commands.description: Create an index on a table column
commands.scenario: Create an index on a table column

commands.command: ALTER TABLE employees ADD CONSTRAINT emp_pk PRIMARY KEY (id);
commands.description: Add a primary key constraint to a table
commands.scenario: Add a primary key constraint to a table

commands.command: ALTER TABLE employees DROP CONSTRAINT emp_pk;
commands.description: Remove a constraint from a table
commands.scenario: Remove a constraint from a table

commands.command: SELECT employees.name, departments.name FROM employees INNER JOIN departments ON employees.department_id = departments.id;
commands.description: Perform an inner join between two tables
commands.scenario: Perform an inner join between two tables

commands.command: SELECT name FROM employees WHERE department_id IN (SELECT id FROM departments WHERE name = 'Sales');
commands.description: Use a subquery to filter results
commands.scenario: Use a subquery to filter results

commands.command: SELECT employees.name, departments.name FROM employees LEFT JOIN departments ON employees.department_id = departments.id;
commands.description: Perform a left join between two tables
commands.scenario: Perform a left join between two tables

commands.description: Set a savepoint in a transaction
commands.scenario: Set a savepoint in a transaction
